description: Fix four bugs across the CLI and plugin-tools packages: tilde expansion missing in localPlugins add/remove commands, resolvePluginPath missing tilde handling, unhandled promise rejection on spawn failure in start command, and source map invalidation from post-esbuild hashAndFreeze injection.

userStories.title: Fix tilde expansion in localPlugins add and remove commands
userStories.description: As a user, I want `opentabs config set localPlugins.add ~/my-plugin` to correctly resolve to my home directory so that the plugin is found at the correct absolute path.
acceptanceCriteria: handleSetLocalPluginsAdd expands ~/... to homedir()/... before calling resolve()
handleSetLocalPluginsRemove expands ~/... to homedir()/... before calling resolve()
`opentabs config set localPlugins.remove ~/projects/my-plugin` correctly matches an entry stored as the expanded path

userStories.title: Fix resolvePluginPath to handle tilde prefix
userStories.description: As a user, I want plugin paths stored as '~/my-plugin' in config.json to resolve correctly when used by doctor and plugin commands so that they are consistent with the config set command.
acceptanceCriteria: resolvePluginPath in platform/cli/src/config.ts handles ~/... paths by expanding to homedir()
Absolute paths and relative paths continue to resolve correctly

userStories.title: Fix unhandled promise rejection in start.ts spawnStreaming
userStories.description: As a user, I want `opentabs start` to show a clean error message when the node binary cannot be found so that I don't see an unhandled promise rejection warning.
acceptanceCriteria: The exited promise from spawnStreaming does not cause an unhandled rejection when the child process fails to spawn
If spawn fails (e.g., ENOENT), the error is caught and a user-friendly message is shown
userStories.notes:
File: platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts, lines 54-57 and 281-292.

The bug: At line 54, the `exited` promise is created with `child.on('error', reject)`. If the child fails to spawn (e.g., node binary not found), the 'error' event fires synchronously, rejecting `exited` immediately. But the code at lines 281-289 first awaits stream pipes (`Promise.all([stdoutPipe, stderrPipe])`) before awaiting `proc.exited` at line 292. The unhandled rejection fires during the gap.

Fix: Add a no-op catch immediately after creating the proc to prevent unhandled rejection:
```typescript
const proc = spawnStreaming(platformExec('node'), [serverEntry], { env, stdin: 'inherit' });
// Prevent unhandled rejection if spawn fails before we await proc.exited
proc.exited.catch(() => {});
```

The error will still be surfaced when `await proc.exited` runs at line 292 (re-throws the rejection). The `.catch(() => {})` just prevents the Node.js unhandled rejection warning in the gap.

Alternative: Use `Promise.allSettled` to await all three concurrently:
```typescript
const [, , exitResult] = await Promise.all([stdoutPipe, stderrPipe, proc.exited.catch(e => { throw e; })]);
```
But the simpler `.catch(() => {})` is cleaner since we still need sequential behavior (streams end, then check exit code).

userStories.title: Fix source map invalidation from hashAndFreeze injection
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want source maps in the adapter IIFE to have correct line mappings so that stack traces and breakpoints in Chrome DevTools align with the actual TypeScript source.
acceptanceCriteria: The hashAndFreeze snippet is included in the esbuild bundle (via footer or banner option) so that esbuild accounts for it in the source map
OR the source map's mappings are adjusted after injection to account for the added lines
OR the hashAndFreeze is injected on the same line as the end of the IIFE (no extra newlines) so line mappings are not shifted
Source map file (.map) correctly maps lines after the injection point
userStories.notes:
File: platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts, lines 1065-1093.

The bug: At line 1065, esbuild bundles the IIFE and generates a source map with line mappings for the original output. Then at line 1090-1093, a hashAndFreeze snippet (2 lines: 1 blank + 1 code) is inserted between the IIFE content and the sourceMappingURL comment. This shifts all lines after the insertion point, but the .map file's `mappings` field still references the original line numbers.

Result: When debugging in Chrome DevTools, breakpoints and stack traces for code after the injection point are off by ~2 lines.

Best fix approach: The hashAndFreeze snippet currently starts with `\n` (line 1090). Change it to NOT add a leading newline — instead, keep it on the same line as the end of the IIFE. Since the IIFE ends with `})();` and the hashAndFreeze starts with `(function(){...`, they can be concatenated without a newline. This avoids shifting source map line numbers entirely.

Change line 1090 from:
```
const hashAndFreeze = `\n(function(){...`;
```
to:
```
const hashAndFreeze = `(function(){...`;
```

The esbuild IIFE output ends with `})();\n`. The `\n` after the IIFE content is already stripped by the regex on line 1072. So the concatenation at line 1093 would be: `iifeContent` (no trailing newline) + `hashAndFreeze` (no leading newline, single line) + `sourceMappingUrlSuffix` (starts with `\n`). This keeps everything on the same line as the IIFE's last line, preserving source map alignment.

IMPORTANT: The hashAndFreeze must remain a single line (no newlines within it). It already is a single line at line 1091. Just remove the leading `\n` from line 1090.

Note: the bundleIIFE function starts around line 683. Read it to understand the esbuild config (especially sourcemap and footer options).
